brief_justification: Arrives at exact correct answer (No) with fully valid temporal lag reasoning and causal chain; multi-level analysis traces corruption inflow vs. discovery reservoir vs. complaint pipeline as distinct layers, plus second-order CEO error decomposition; no explicit meta-commentary on reasoning method or named biases—proceeds linearly without checking own assumptions; explicit counterfactual verification via 'what a failed fix looks like' section with three falsifying conditions; acknowledges temporal uncertainty ('roughly Day 5–10', 'Day 14+') but does not structure confidence levels or enumerate assumptions; considers failed-fix alternative scenario and CEO's erroneous interpretation but not other competing explanations (new bug, reporting changes); audit trail is fully reproducible—table maps observation days to corruption dates, causal chain is traceable end-to-end; avoids early-anchor bias (treats rising complaints as expected, not as fix failure) but does not name primacy bias explicitly as the anti-pattern being avoided; no evidence of suppressed failure modes—no primacy bias, no environment-as-passive assumption, no anchor to surface observation; shows branched structure (temporal audit → reservoir model → counterfactual → CEO error) but does not closely follow the scan-strip-substitute contradiction-resolution topology.
